description_long: This is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a cylindrical metal pole, featuring three circular signal lights arranged vertically. The housing is rectangular with slightly rounded edges and displays visible wear and weathering, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The three signal lenses are colored red, yellow, and green from top to bottom, each set within a recessed, hooded compartment to shield from sunlight glare. The pole is robust and textured, likely made of painted or powder-coated metal, and is designed to be anchored securely into the ground. The back of the housing is flat and featureless, while the sides reveal the protruding visors over each light. No pedestrian signals or control boxes are visible, and the overall design is utilitarian, optimized for visibility and durability in urban environments.
description: A weathered, pole-mounted traffic light with three vertically stacked signal lights—red, yellow, and green—housed in a rectangular metal casing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the depth of the housing and the protruding visors over each of the three signal lights.
description_view_1: Front view displaying all three circular signal lights—red, yellow, and green—stacked vertically within the rectangular housing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the depth and the individual visors for each light.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat, featureless back of the traffic light housing and the cylindrical pole.
